findings.description: Judith A. Broyles (b. 1869) is a full sister of Charles Robinson Broyles — both children of George Broyles and Mary Elizabeth Davis. She appears in the George Broyles household in the 1880 U.S. Census (Marshall, Saline, Missouri).
supporting_sources: 1880 U.S. Census — George Broyles household, Marshall, Saline, Missouri (Judith enumerated as a child alongside Charles)

findings.description: Minnie Belle Broyles (b. 28 Feb 1877) is a full sister of Charles Robinson Broyles — both children of George Broyles and Mary Elizabeth Davis. She appears in the George Broyles household in the 1880 U.S. Census (Marshall, Saline, Missouri).
supporting_sources: 1880 U.S. Census — George Broyles household, Marshall, Saline, Missouri (Minnie enumerated as a child alongside Charles)

findings.description: Myrtle Leana Broyles (b. 17 May 1886) is a full sister of Charles Robinson Broyles — both children of George Broyles and Mary Elizabeth Davis. Born after the 1880 census, she is recoverable from a later (e.g. 1900) census of the household or Missouri birth records.
supporting_sources: 1900 U.S. Census of the George/Mary Broyles household; Missouri birth records naming the same parents

findings.description: William Harrison Broyles (b. 4 Sep 1857) is a half-brother of Charles Robinson Broyles — a son of George Broyles by his first wife (Liddy, m. 1856), so they share a father. Recoverable via the 1860 census of the first-marriage household.
supporting_sources: 1860 U.S. Census — George Broyles household, Palestine Township, Cooper, Missouri (George's first marriage)

findings.description: Milton M. Broyles (b. 1859) is a half-brother of Charles Robinson Broyles — a son of George Broyles by his first wife (Liddy), sharing a father. Recoverable via the 1860 census of the first-marriage household.
supporting_sources: 1860 U.S. Census — George Broyles household, Palestine Township, Cooper, Missouri (George's first marriage)

findings.description: Geo. W. Broyles (b. 1861) is a half-brother of Charles Robinson Broyles — a son of George Broyles by his first wife (Liddy), sharing a father. Recoverable via the 1870 census of the first-marriage household.
supporting_sources: 1870 U.S. Census — George Broyles household, Missouri (George's first marriage)
